---
title: heval
---

Health economic evaluation in Python: parameter specification and probabilistic sampling, simulation across model types, cost-effectiveness analysis, and value-of-information analysis for model-based HEOR and HTA work.

## The core idea

One parameter object flows through swappable model engines into a shared analysis layer. Engines do not share an implementation API; they share a contract on their outputs. Given a matrix of parameter draws, every engine returns costs and effects per strategy per iteration in one standardized structure, `Outcomes`, a tidy frame indexed by `(strategy, iteration)`. Standardized outputs make CEA and VoI engine-agnostic.

Two invariants hold everywhere. First, the outcome schema is the integration point: every engine targets it, every analysis consumes it, and none reaches into engine internals. Second, parameter and outcome matrices share the iteration index, so EVPPI and EVSI can trace which draw produced which outcome; `run_psa` enforces it. [Architecture](concepts/architecture.qmd) covers both in detail.

## Quickstart

A PSA table from any source enters the pipeline through one call:

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from heval.run import as_outcomes
from heval.cea import icer_table
from heval.voi import evpi

rng = np.random.default_rng(7)
n = 2_000
df = pd.concat(
    pd.DataFrame({
        "strategy": name, "iteration": range(n),
        "cost": rng.normal(cost, 2_000, n), "qaly": rng.normal(q, 0.4, n),
    })
    for name, cost, q in [("Standard care", 40_000, 8.0), ("New drug", 52_000, 8.6)]
)

outcomes = as_outcomes(df)
icer_table(outcomes).round(1)
```

```{python}
print(f"EVPI at WTP 30,000: {evpi(outcomes, wtp=30_000):,.1f}")
```

## Where to go next

- [Get started](get-started.qmd): install and run your first analysis.
- [Bring your own outputs](tutorials/byo-outputs.qmd): an external PSA CSV through CEA, VoI, plots, and a model card.
- [Full pipeline](tutorials/full-pipeline.qmd): parameters, `run_psa`, CEA, and VoI end to end.
- [API reference](reference/index.qmd): every public function, generated from docstrings.
- [Roadmap](roadmap/index.md) and [changelog](changelog.md): what is planned and what has shipped.
